---
title: "Exercise 01 — Bash Testing with bats"
format: html
toc: true
---

::: {.callout-note}
🇫🇷 [Version française](../../../exercises/01_bash/README.qmd)
:::

## Objective

Write bats tests for a Bash script that validates a TSV file.

## Quick Reference — bats

Documentation: <https://bats-core.readthedocs.io/en/stable/>

| Element | Syntax |
|---------|---------|
| Run a command | `run bash script.sh arg` |
| Exit code | `[ "$status" -eq 0 ]` |
| Output content | `[[ "$output" =~ "pattern" ]]` |
| Individual line | `[ "${lines[0]}" = "texte" ]` |

---

## Context

Before running a pipeline on a data file, we want to verify:

- that the file exists
- that it is not empty
- that the header is correct (`id`, `group`, `value`)
- that the `value` column is numeric

The script to test is `pipelines/minimal/scripts/validate_records.sh`.

## Data

```bash
training/data/records.tsv          # Valid TSV (20 rows)
training/data/empty.tsv            # Header only, no data
training/data/invalid_records.tsv  # Non-numeric value
```

## Starting Code

The `validate_records.sh` script:

```bash
#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -euo pipefail

FILE="${1:-}"

[ -n "$FILE" ] || { echo "ERROR: usage: $0 <records.tsv>"; exit 1; }
[ -f "$FILE" ] || { echo "ERROR: file not found: $FILE"; exit 1; }

lines=$(wc -l < "$FILE")
if [ "$lines" -lt 2 ]; then
    echo "ERROR: file is empty or has no data rows: $FILE"
    exit 1
fi
# ... vérifie header, colonnes, valeurs numériques
echo "OK: N records validated"
```

## Exercise

Complete the file `tests/bats/test_validate_records.bats`:

```bash
#!/usr/bin/env bats

SCRIPT="training/pipelines/minimal/scripts/validate_records.sh"
DATA="training/data"

@test "fichier valide → exit 0" {
    # TODO : tester avec records.tsv
}

@test "fichier vide → exit 1" {
    # TODO : tester avec empty.tsv
}

@test "valeur non numérique → exit 1" {
    # TODO : tester avec invalid_records.tsv
}

@test "fichier inexistant → exit 1 et message" {
    # TODO : tester avec un chemin qui n'existe pas
}

@test "aucun argument → exit 1 et usage" {
    # TODO : appeler le script sans argument
}
```

## Expected Result

```
✓ fichier valide → exit 0 et 20 records
✓ fichier vide (en-tête seul) → exit 1
✓ valeur non numérique → exit 1
✓ fichier inexistant → exit 1 et message
✓ aucun argument → exit 1 et usage

5 tests, 0 failures
```

## Solution

→ See [solution.qmd](solution.qmd)
